entries.source: A prior response already allowed this command and saved the choice.
A prior response already allowed this command once.
Gateway recorded approval and saved the choice.
A prior response already denied this approval.
This approval expired before it could be resolved.
This approval was cancelled before it could be resolved.
A prior response already resolved this approval.
Could not load Gateway talk catalog
No ${issue.target.title} provider is configured on the Gateway
Configure a ${issue.target.title} provider on the Gateway
Gateway did not identify the active ${issue.target.title} provider
Choose a supported ${issue.target.title} provider on the Gateway
Too many shares are waiting to be added.
Resolution outcome unknown. Actions stay disabled until the Gateway record is verified.
The Gateway still shows this approval as pending. Review it before trying again.
Could not load approval details. Refresh and try again.
Could not resolve approval. Refresh and try again.
Failed: no secure gateway endpoint was detected. Enable gateway TLS or Tailscale Serve, or use a trusted private LAN address with Unencrypted selected.
Failed: secure endpoint reached, but TLS fingerprint verification timed out. Check Tailscale Serve or gateway TLS and retry.
Failed: couldn't reach the secure gateway endpoint for this host.
Update your Gateway to view provider model config.
Could not load provider model config.
Please enable ${labels.joinToString(", ")} in Android Settings to continue.
Current screen output and interactive app surface.
Canvas output needs an active gateway connection.
Open the current Canvas surface to inspect or interact with it.
Messaging surfaces connected to this gateway.
Connect the gateway to load channels.
Telegram, WhatsApp, email, and other channels appear here after setup.
Some channel status checks did not complete.
Try Chat, Voice, Sessions, Providers, or Settings.
Connect the Gateway to search sessions.
This permanently removes the scheduled job from the gateway.
This job changed while you were editing. Revert to the latest gateway version before saving.
Save or revert your edits before running, enabling, disabling, deleting, or refreshing this job.

Cron changes require operator.admin. Setup codes intentionally do not grant it. Reconnect with the gateway's shared token or password to request admin access. If this device still lacks it, approve the pending scope upgrade from an existing admin client.
Allow the scheduler to run this job.
Remove this job after a successful one-shot run.
The gateway can change this path but cannot clear an existing path.
Connect the gateway to load dreaming.
Entries appear after a dreaming cycle writes a narrative summary.
The diary is waiting for its first entry.
Setup code points to an insecure remote gateway. $remoteGatewaySecurityRule $remoteGatewaySecurityFix
QR code points to an insecure remote gateway. $remoteGatewaySecurityRule $remoteGatewaySecurityFix
Setup code uses an IPv6 zone ID. Use an unscoped IPv6 address or a LAN hostname.
QR code uses an IPv6 zone ID. Use an unscoped IPv6 address or a LAN hostname.
IPv6 zone IDs are not supported. Use an unscoped IPv6 address or a LAN hostname.
Setup code has invalid gateway URL.
QR code did not contain a valid setup code.
Enter a valid manual endpoint to connect.
Gateway status, phone node readiness, and recent log stream.
Connect the gateway to load recent logs.
Live nodes, paired phones, and pending device requests.
Connect the gateway to load nodes and paired devices.
Linked phones and node hosts will appear here after pairing.

This gateway sign-in can list connected nodes, but it cannot approve new phone pairing. Pair new phones from a gateway admin session. Node capability approval is separate and still uses nodes approve <request id>.
Turn this device into a secure OpenClaw node for chat, voice, camera, and device tools.
The connected OpenClaw agent can use device capabilities you enable. Continue only if you trust the Gateway and agent you connect to.
Scan a QR code or use the setup code from your OpenClaw Gateway.
Have a terminal open on the device running OpenClaw.
Use the same network, or a secure remote Gateway URL.
Open the camera and frame the code from openclaw qr.
Align the QR code inside the square.
Camera access is needed to scan the setup QR.
Use the Gateway computer's LAN address or secure remote hostname.
Paste a shared Gateway token or operator-issued token.
Gateway pairing is complete. Approve this phone as a node so OpenClaw can use the device capabilities you enable.
Use the requestId from the pending command in the approve command.
Run the approve command on the Gateway computer, then check again.
Only enable access you are comfortable letting OpenClaw use while this phone is connected. You can change these later in Android Settings.
Gateway approval is in progress. OpenClaw will retry automatically.
Gateway paired. Checking node capability approval.
The code may have expired or been generated for another Gateway.
Gateway password is required. Enter it again or edit this connection.
Gateway password is invalid. Re-enter it or reset this gateway connection.
Gateway token is required. Enter it again or edit this connection.
Gateway requires this device identity. Re-authenticate or reset this gateway connection.
Saved authentication is invalid. Re-authenticate or reset this gateway connection.
Gateway authentication is not configured. Edit this connection and try again.
Gateway authentication needs review. Check gateway settings, then retry.

Review provider readiness
and configured models.

$modelCount configured models. Refresh to recheck availability.

This permanently deletes the session and its transcript.
Start a new conversation and it will show up here.

Archived sessions will show up here.

Provider limits will appear here when your gateway reports them.
Scheduled OpenClaw work from your gateway.
Open a job to inspect its configuration and run history. Admin-scoped connections can also run, edit, enable, disable, or delete it.
Connect the gateway to load cron jobs.

Connect the gateway to inspect cron jobs.
Choose and inspect the assistants available on this gateway.

How this phone appears to OpenClaw.

Showing ${visibleApps.size} of ${apps.size}. Refine search for more.
Choose what this phone can share.
Selected or full photo access granted.
App list stays on this phone.
Always allows requested location checks while OpenClaw is in the background; Android shows this in the persistent node notification.
OpenClaw only checks location when your paired Gateway requests it. On the next Android screen, choose $backgroundPermissionLabel to allow checks while the app is in the background.

OpenClaw collects and sends the names, package IDs, and status of apps visible on this phone when your paired OpenClaw Gateway asks for them. This lets your assistant answer questions and take actions using installed apps.
Your phone sends this information to your Gateway, not to a server run by OpenClaw. Your Gateway may include it in requests to the AI provider you chose.
Remove ${entry?.name ?: "this gateway"} and its saved credentials from this phone?
Connection between this phone and OpenClaw.
Scan or paste a setup code to add another gateway.

Android can scan or paste an existing setup code, but this gateway does not expose setup-code generation to the app yet. Generate the QR/code on the gateway host with openclaw qr, then scan it here or paste the setup code below.
Enter a valid setup code or gateway address.
Changes Android text that OpenClaw has translated. Screens with English-only copy stay unchanged.
OpenClaw appreciates its partners in the open-source community.
No license notices are packaged in this build.

Replacing the setup code clears this phone's saved setup credentials and device tokens before reconnecting. This phone may need node capability approval again; continue only when you mean to pair with a fresh gateway setup code.
OpenClaw turns this phone into a clean mobile command surface for sessions, voice, providers, and Gateway.
A Gateway update is available. Run the update from the Web UI or CLI when you are ready.
No apps selected. Nothing forwards until you add apps.
No apps blocked. Apps can forward unless you add blocks.
Start a chat and your active OpenClaw conversations will appear here.
Review generated skill proposals before they become live skills.
Connect to a Gateway to load Skill Workshop proposals.
Matching proposals will appear here after agents create reusable skill drafts.
This will ${action.action.label.lowercase()} "${action.title}" and refresh Skill Workshop state from the gateway.
Apply, reject, and quarantine require operator.admin scope. Reconnect with shared gateway auth or approve an operator.admin device scope upgrade to enable lifecycle actions.
Skill Workshop inspect and apply actions
Skill Workshop reject and quarantine actions

The watch receives a one-time pairing code and stores its own device token. A reachable secure Gateway URL is required away from the iPhone.

Discovered gateways and manual setup live here when the gateway has not connected yet.
Review this exec request before continuing. Your decision will be sent back to the gateway.
Enable Gateway TLS, or enter your Tailscale Serve HTTPS host in Manual Setup. Use Unencrypted only with a trusted private-LAN address.

Exec approvals can only be reviewed while OpenClaw is open and connected.

Enable Notifications to receive approval notifications while OpenClaw is not open.
OpenClaw uses your calendars to show events and scheduling context when you enable calendar access.
OpenClaw uses your calendars to add events when you enable calendar access.

OpenClaw uses the camera when you scan a Gateway setup QR code or ask your paired Gateway or assistant to capture a photo or short video from this iPhone, for example to connect to your Gateway or show your assistant a document, device screen, or workspace.
OpenClaw uses your contacts so you can search and reference people while using the assistant.
OpenClaw discovers and connects to your OpenClaw gateway on the local network.
OpenClaw can share your location in the background when you enable Always.
OpenClaw uses your location when you allow location sharing.
OpenClaw uses the microphone for realtime chat, voice wake, push-to-talk, and voice notes.
OpenClaw may use motion data to support device-aware interactions and automations.
OpenClaw lets your assistant read photos you allow and lets you choose photos to share.
OpenClaw uses your reminders to list, add, and complete tasks when you enable reminders access.
OpenClaw uses on-device speech recognition for talk mode and voice wake.
Location sharing is disabled in OpenClaw. Location Services are off in iOS Settings.
Location sharing is disabled in OpenClaw. iOS currently allows Always.
Location sharing is disabled in OpenClaw. iOS currently allows While Using.
Location Services are off in iOS Settings.

Foreground location requests are allowed. Precise Location is off.

Background location requests and significant-change updates are allowed. Precise Location is off.
Background location requests and significant-change updates are allowed.

Menu bar companion for notifications, screenshots, and privileged agent actions.

This copy is newer than the installed app. OpenClaw will replace it and reopen from Applications.
OpenClaw will copy itself to Applications and reopen there so updates and launch at login stay reliable.
OpenClaw is installed in Applications, but couldn’t reopen automatically. Open it there manually.
Copy cookies from \(browsers) into an isolated agent profile. Passwords are never touched.
Copying \(profile.displayName) into “\(target)”. Touch ID may be required.

This tab is read-only in Nix mode. Edit config via Nix and rebuild.
Wildcard config entries are edited from their concrete key.
Your AI-powered setup helper. It can check status, fix config, switch models, and connect channels.
Tip: try “status”, “doctor”, “set default model …”, or “connect telegram”.
Optional override (phone number / chat id / Discord channel)

This process looks expected for the current mode. Kill anyway?
When enabled, OpenClaw won't install or manage \(gatewayLaunchdLabel). It will only attach to an existing Gateway.
Tools for diagnosing local issues (Gateway, ports, logs, Canvas).
Deep links (openclaw://…) are always enabled; the key controls unattended runs.
Controls the macOS app log verbosity.
Writes a rotating, local-only log under ~/Library/Logs/OpenClaw/. Enable only while actively debugging.
Check which process owns \(GatewayEnvironment.gatewayPort()) and suggest fixes.
Used for pnpm/node fallback and PATH population when launching the gateway.
Used by the CLI session loader; stored in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.

Uses the Voice Wake path: forwards over SSH when configured,
otherwise runs locally via rpc.

Let an authorized agent move the pointer, click, and type on this Mac. Also requires Accessibility, Screen Recording, and gateway command authorization. High risk.

Local is best for this Mac. Remote is best when the Gateway already runs on a Mac Studio or server.

SSH keeps the Gateway private; direct is best for HTTPS or Tailscale Serve.

The WebSocket URL exposed by the remote Gateway.
Use wss:// for public hosts. ws:// is allowed for localhost, LAN, .local, and Tailnet hosts.

Your personal AI assistant, living on your own Mac.
It answers questions, works with your files and apps, and can chat with you on WhatsApp or Telegram. Setup takes about two minutes.
Give your assistant tasks and let it help across your Mac.
This app, WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack — your choice.
Everything runs where you decide, with permissions you grant.
OpenClaw can take actions using the permissions and services you enable. Review prompts and only connect tools you trust.
Most people pick this Mac — OpenClaw installs everything and keeps it running in the background. You can change this anytime in Settings.
Skip Gateway setup for now; pick Local or Remote later in Settings → General.
OK — OpenClaw won’t start anything yet. Pick Local or Remote later in Settings → General.

\(count) gateways found on your network — click to choose one.

Tip: keep Tailscale enabled so your gateway stays reachable.

These macOS permissions let OpenClaw automate apps and capture context on this Mac. Status updates automatically.
OpenClaw is setting up its background service on this Mac. This usually takes under a minute — no Terminal, no administrator password.
Runs quietly and starts again after a restart.
Once the service answers, you’ll connect your AI.

(typically `~/.openclaw/credentials/oauth.json`). Then connect again if needed.

Open the menu bar panel for quick chat; the agent can show previews and richer visuals in Canvas.
Enable optional skills (Peekaboo, oracle, camsnap, …) from Settings → Skills.

Make sure the Gateway is running and connected, then hit Refresh (or open Settings → Skills).

macOS access for notifications, capture, voice, and device context.

This gateway requires an auth token
That token did not match the gateway
This gateway host needs token setup
This setup code is no longer valid
This gateway is using unsupported auth
Paste the token configured on the gateway host. On the gateway host, run `openclaw config get gateway.auth.token`. If the gateway uses an environment variable instead, use `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN`.
Check `gateway.auth.token` or `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` on the gateway host and try again.

This gateway is set to token auth, but no `gateway.auth.token` is configured on the gateway host. If the gateway uses an environment variable instead, set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` before starting the gateway.
Scan or paste a fresh setup code from an already-paired OpenClaw client, then try again.
This onboarding flow does not support password auth yet. Reconfigure the gateway to use token auth, then retry.
Approve this device from an already-paired OpenClaw client. In your OpenClaw chat, run `/pair approve`, then click **Check connection** again.
This gateway requires an auth token from the gateway host.
Gateway token mismatch. Check gateway.auth.token or OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN on the gateway host.
This gateway has token auth enabled, but no gateway.auth.token is configured on the host.
Setup code expired or already used. Scan a fresh setup code, then try again.
This gateway uses password auth. Remote onboarding on macOS cannot collect gateway passwords yet.
Pairing required. In an already-paired OpenClaw client, run /pair approve, then check the connection again.
ssh tunnel did not open local port \(localPort)
OpenClaw needs notification permission to show alerts for agent actions.
OpenClaw captures the screen when the agent needs screenshots for context.
OpenClaw can capture photos or short video clips when requested by the agent.
OpenClaw can share your location when requested by the agent.
OpenClaw uses the local network to connect to your remote OpenClaw gateway over LAN, Tailnet, or SSH.
OpenClaw needs the mic for Voice Wake tests and agent audio capture.
OpenClaw uses speech recognition to detect your Voice Wake trigger phrase.
OpenClaw needs Automation (AppleScript) permission to drive Terminal and other apps for agent actions.

Wake phrases, push-to-talk, microphone input, and Talk Mode feedback.

Play short sounds when Talk Mode switches between listening, thinking, and speaking.

Short phrases that start voice wake detection.
Languages are tried in order. Models may need a first-use download on macOS 26.
OpenClaw reacts when any trigger appears in a transcription. Keep phrases short to avoid false positives.

INSERT INTO cached_transcripts(gateway_id, session_key, agent_id, payload, updated_at)
SELECT gateway_id, session_key, agent_id, payload, updated_at
FROM cached_transcripts_pre_v3

INSERT INTO cached_transcripts(gateway_id, session_key, agent_id, payload, updated_at)
SELECT gateway_id, session_key, '', payload, updated_at
FROM cached_transcripts_pre_v3
WHERE lower(trim(session_key)) GLOB 'agent:*:*'
